Fit + smooth the residual for one analyte over a (clipped) daily grid

Source: R/kalman_bridge.R
dot-residual_smoother.Rd

Clips target_dates to the analyte's grab span [first_anchor, last_anchor] (no extrapolation beyond the grabs), fits the OU parameters, builds the KFAS model with optional hydrology-modulated process variance, and returns the smoothed posterior. For degenerate series returns a flat mean with zero variance.

Usage

.residual_smoother(
  anchor_dates,
  anchor_S,
  target_dates,
  z_hydro = NULL,
  kappa = 0.5,
  n_fit_min = 8L,
  scale = 1,
  r_vec = NULL
)

Arguments

anchor_dates, anchor_S

Observed anchors.

target_dates

Candidate daily grid (will be clipped to the grab span).

z_hydro

Optional standardised hydro feature aligned to target_dates (same length); drives q_mult = exp(kappa * z). NULL -> no modulation.

kappa

Hydrology process-variance sensitivity (default 0.5).

n_fit_min, scale

Passed to .estimate_ou_kalman_params().

r_vec

Per-anchor observation variance (default tiny).

Value

list(grid_dates, params, model, mean, var). model is NULL for the degenerate path.
